channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: 'Larger budgets and longer schedules do not lead to better games:' Thank You For Your Application recoups dev costs in 22 hours
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

June 22, 2026

1 Min Read

Thank You For Your Application has recouped its development costs in just 22 hours. The narrative-driven interview simulator is the brainchild of IceLemonTea Studio, a cohort of young developers fresh out of university. 

Publisher No More Robots shared the news and told Game Developer the title had a five-figure budget including development costs, localization, and marketing. 

"Production started in January 2025. It was due to release at the start of this year but we pushed it back a little when we realized how big it was going to be, so that we could polish the crap out of it," said No More Robots founder Mike Rose. 

"I've said this many, many times before, but larger budgets and longer schedules do not lead to better games, and the industry as a whole really needs to finally learn that lesson."

Thank You For Your Application launched on Steam and is slated to arrive on consoles and mobile later this year. It debuted on the PC storefront with a 15 percent introductory discount, lowing the price of admission to £12.74 from £14.99.

Related:Obituary: Ubisoft co-founder Claude Guillemot dies in plane crash

No More Robots previously revealed the project had amassed over 135,000 wishlists on Steam prior to launch. 

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/-larger-budgets-and-longer-schedules-do-not-lead-to-better-games-thank-you-for-your-application-recoups-dev-costs-in-22-hours
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Obituary: Ubisoft co-founder Claude Guillemot dies in plane crash
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

June 22, 2026

1 Min Read

Ubisoft co-founder and board member Claude Guillemot has passed away.

Reuters reports (via French newspaper Ouest-France) that Guillemot was one of two people aboard a twin-engine Cessna 421 plane that crashed in the resort town of La Baule, France. 

Both people onboard the aircraft died in the incident. Ubisoft confirmed the news in a brief statement. 

"Ubisoft ‌was deeply saddened to learn of the death of Claude Guillemot, co-founder of the group and chairman of Guillemot Corp, in an accident," said the company. "Our thoughts are with his family and loved ones during this difficult time. No further statements will be made at this time." 

Claude Guillemot established the Assassin's Creed maker in 1986 alongside his brothers Yves, Michel, Gérard and Christian. Yves Guillemot currently leads the French publisher as CEO.

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/obituary-ubisoft-co-founder-claude-guillemot-killed-in-plane-crash
